---
title: ChEMBL
alias: &ALIAS chembl
href: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chembl/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

ChEMBL (EMBL-EBI) is a manually curated database of bioactive drug-like molecules with quantitative information on their target binding, ADMET properties, and pharmacology.

## Key Features

- Contains over 2 million bioactive compounds with experimentally determined activity data (Ki, IC50, MIC)
- Links compounds to protein targets, assay descriptions, and source literature
- Provides drug approval status, mechanism of action, and indication data for approved drugs
- Offers ADMET prediction annotations and Lipinski rule-of-five filtering capabilities
- Supports REST API, Python client (chembl_webresource_client), and bulk data downloads

## Use Cases in Research

- Compiling MIC and activity data for antimicrobials to train ML models for antibiotic discovery
- Identifying compounds with activity against specific bacterial or fungal targets
- Performing QSAR analysis using curated bioactivity data for drug optimization
- Mapping approved antibiotic pharmacology to resistance mechanisms for repurposing studies
- Integrating ChEMBL target data with genomic AMR databases for systems-level analysis

## Getting Started

Visit [ChEMBL](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chembl/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
